heading: Eminent domain
label: 1403 Eminent domain
text: Proceedings to condemn real estate for the use of the United States or its departments or agencies shall be brought in the district court of the district where the land is located or, if located in different districts in the same State, in any of such districts.
notes: Historical and Revision NotesBased on , U.S.C., 1940 ed., Public Buildings, Property, and Works (, ; , ).section 257 of title 40Aug. 1, 1888, ch. 728, § 125 Stat. 357Mar. 3, 1911, ch. 231, § 29136 Stat. 1167Section constitutes the first clause of the second sentence of section 257, of title 40, U.S.C., 1940 ed. The revised section is expressive of the purpose of such section 257 with necessary changes in phraseology.The jurisdiction provision of , U.S.C., 1940 ed., is incorporated in .section 257 of title 40section 1358 of this titleThe remainder of , U.S.C., 1940 ed., is retained in said title 40.section 257 of title 40Provision with respect to property in different districts was added to conform with .section 1392 of this titleSee, also, which fixes venue of an action involving property in different districts in the same State.section 1392 of this title
